issuesFound.explanation: In faqRegistry.parse() the trigger button’s aria‑expanded is only set when the item is opened; when closed the attribute is omitted, violating the accordion pattern.

Ensure the attribute is always present, defaulting to false:  

```js
const isInitiallyOpen = getAttrBool(el, 'data-faq-open') ||
                         getAttrBool(trigger, 'aria-expanded');
```  

issuesFound.title: ARIA‑expanded not initialised for generic toggle widgets (JS‑E)
issuesFound.explanation: Multiple toggle‑type components (dropdowns, mobile nav, billing‑period selector) rely on JavaScript to set aria‑expanded when the widget opens, but none set a default false value on page load, leaving the attribute absent and breaking accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: For each toggle component, add a one‑time initialisation that sets aria‑expanded to false on its trigger element(s) before any user interaction. Example for dropdowns (see Issue 3) and similarly for mobileNav and pricing as shown in Issues 1 and 4.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.toggle()` function changes the drawer’s visibility but never updates the trigger’s `aria‑expanded` state, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu’s open/closed status.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑pressed on the trigger button

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu trigger click does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: `_onTriggerClick()` toggles the panel visibility via `_openInstance()` / `_closeInstance()` but never updates the button’s `aria‑expanded`, breaking the disclosure pattern.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu triggers lack initial aria‑expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is registered (`register()`), the trigger button is not given an explicit `aria‑expanded="false"` state, so its default is undefined.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The `open(item)` and `close(item)` functions change the panel’s visibility but never set `aria‑expanded` on the associated trigger, violating the accordion pattern.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle does not update aria‑pressed on its switch element
issuesFound.explanation: `_syncToggleState()` adds/removes CSS classes for the visual switch but never mirrors the state with `aria‑pressed`, leaving screen‑reader users without feedback.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: `srUtils.markInvalid()` only adds `aria-describedby` but never sets `aria-invalid="true"`, so validation errors are invisible to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter result count badge updates are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: `_updateCountBadge()` changes the numeric text but does not use a live region, so users of assistive tech are not informed when the number of visible products changes.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger lacks aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In dropdownMenu.register() the trigger button receives aria‑haspopup and aria‑controls but never receives aria‑expanded to reflect the open/closed state of its associated panel. Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the dropdown is open.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion creates each FAQ item’s trigger button with aria‑controls but never sets aria‑expanded to indicate whether the answer panel is visible. This violates the ARIA disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In faqAccordion.open() add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') and in close() add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: Site‑search combobox does not manage aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is given role="combobox" and aria‑controls but the script never updates aria‑expanded when the suggestion list opens or closes, leaving the state ambiguous for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: In siteSearch._open() add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true') and in siteSearch._close() add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: Toggle buttons do not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile‑nav button, the billing‑period toggle (pricing._toggleEl) and the view‑mode buttons use a visual “pressed” state but never set aria‑pressed on the elements, so screen‑reader users cannot perceive the toggle state.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate() marks fields as invalid visually but never adds aria‑invalid="true" or removes it on success, preventing AT from announcing validation errors.

issuesFound.title: FAQ answer expansion not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is opened or closed the script emits custom events but does not send an announcement to a live region, so users of screen readers are unaware of the change.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce update
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the billing period updates price numbers but no live‑region announcement is made, so users of assistive technology may miss the price change.

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not initialised on page load for dropdowns
